ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #6, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Jun-06-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Francesca Callejo for (conservatee) Ernesto Jauregui. Jennifer Steneberg (via court call) for Union Bank.

The Court stated that we are here for approval of the 6th account and report, that there are issues regarding whether it is appropriate to approve the fees requested on a percentage basis and that the second is the apparent request to abandon the advisory committee which seems to be mandated by the trust. Ms. Callejo stated that she was not present when the trust was initiated, that the advisory committee was for the family to have a say, that it has not been successful, that the sisters resigned as co-trustees as they did not think they ware getting anywhere with their brother, that they looked for and appointed someone else who was not successful, that Ms. Dolch was subsequently appointed, that the original concept of the advisory committee was not working as planned, that Ms. Dolch is working to expand Mr. Jauregui's daily activities, that Mr. Jauregui is happy and that they can file a petition to amend the trust. The Court stated that the advisory committee also gave them other powers such as appointing a successor trustee. Ms. Callejo suggested that Ms. Dolch would welcome input from the sisters and that Ms. Dolch could be a part of the committee. Ms. Callejo further stated that there is one sister, Lupe, who is the most involved, that is familiar with his care, that in regards to fees, the trust has gone up in value, that she does not know how much time has been spent, that the Court could request time records and that reducing the fees might discourage the bank from taking this on. Ms. Steneberg stated that in regards to the advisory committee, maintaining it is an issue, that it is ineffective, that there is no meaningful communication with the bank, that her client is concerned, that the bank would be more comfortable with the powers being transferred to Ms. Dolch, that the fees request is based on the 1% guideline and that it is an active case with additional activities that are outside of the norm. Ms. Callejo stated that the expense for the accounting preparation is to be reduced. Ms. Steneberg stated that it is addressed in the amendment. The Court took the accounting petition under submission stating that it will review the order, that in regards to the advisory committee the parties need to talk and agree on a way to proceed and requested that Ms. Steneberg use the Judicial Council form for notices.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)

Petition To Eliminate Requirement To Appoint Additional Trust Advisory Committee Member, And To Approve Attorney'S Fees
Off Calendar Dec-22-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jennifer Steneberg for MUGF Union Bank. Franchesca Callejo for Conservatee.

Ms. Callejo stated that her Client is not interested in receiving additional services. Ms. Steneberg stated that the membership advisory committee is willing to hire a case manager as needed. Ms. Callejo stated that her Client could benefit from a review at this point. Ms. Steneberg also stated that is typical for her firm to charge for travel time, that a $600.00 reduction is appropriate and addressed the $390.00 charge for file and serve. The Petition to Eliminate Requirement to Appoint Additional Trust Advisory Member and to Approve Attorney's Fees was taken under submission. Status on replacement of a case manager member of the advisory committee was set for January 26, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Paul Alvarado. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #5, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Aug-11-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jennifer Stenberg for (petitioner) United Bank, N.A.

Ms. Stenberg stated that fees to the Pomeroy Center vary due to the conservatee's attendance, that she will work with Ms. Callejo and that she hopes to have more communication with the family members to engage the conservatee in more activities. The Court approved the accounting petition (subject to review of the order).

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Leslie Gomez/Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #5, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Jul-22-2014 Continued To Aug-05-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jennifer Stenberg for Union Bank.

Ms. Stenberg stated that she was not sure about the Cobra circumstances, that Mr. Jauregui is currently covered by Medi-Cal and will continue to see his physicians at Kaiser. Ms. Stenberg also stated that she was not aware that Ms. Callejo was court appointed in the conservatorship, that she will look into the issue of whether or not Ernesto has been attending the Janet Pomeroy Center and that they are investigating this issue and that the trustee is fully prepared to stop making payments to the center. The accounting petition was continued to August 5, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for counsel to advise Ms. Callejo and file a brief regarding the Pomeroy Center. Said brief is due by July 31, 2014.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera/Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico. (Not Reported)
